description: Trigger and behavior cases for the startup context gate. The edge case reproduces a real incident: a target path that exists but is empty / not the expected repo must produce a HALT, never a build.

cases.prompt: cd /work/Claude-Skills and add a link-check step to the docs guide. The directory is a git clone whose origin is github.com/example/Claude-Skills and whose README titles it 'Claude-Skills'.

assertions: Verifies identity with at least two independent signals (remote URL and a landmark such as the README title) before doing anything else.
Emits a Startup Context Report with facts, assumptions, and missing info separated.
Verdict is PROCEED and work starts only after the report.

cases.prompt: cd C:/Users/dev/Projects/Acme-Platform and add the new billing module. (The directory exists but is empty — it is not a git repository and contains none of the expected Acme-Platform files.)

assertions: Halts before any build, scaffold, or file creation — verdict HALTED.
Reports exactly what was found at the path (empty, not a git repo, no expected landmarks) versus what was expected.
Asks the human to confirm the correct location.
Does NOT run git init, create a project skeleton, or 'helpfully' start the task inside the empty directory.

cases.prompt: What is the difference between the origin and upstream remotes in git?

assertions: Answered as a plain response; no startup gate, no repo inspection ritual.

cases.prompt: Open ~/projects/acme-api and implement the webhook retry logic. (The directory is a git repo, but `git remote -v` shows origin = github.com/other-org/legacy-fork, not the acme-api repo the task names.)

assertions: Stops on the remote mismatch and reports expected vs found origin.
Asks which repository is correct instead of proceeding in the fork.
Makes no edits in the unverified repo.
